articles.title: Crown Holdings lifts 2026 outlook on aluminium can demand
articles.summary: Crown Holdings reported Q2 2026 adjusted diluted EPS of $2.49, up 16% year-on-year, as global beverage can volumes grew 5% including 7% in Europe and 5% in North America; the company raised its full-year 2026 EPS guidance to $8.30–$8.50 and announced new can-making capacity expansions in Brazil, Greece, Spain, and India.
articles.novelis_angle: European can volume growth of 7% and Crown’s planned capacity additions in Greece and Spain strengthen the near-term demand signal for Novelis can body sheet across Southern European markets.

articles.title: Washington unveils aluminium onshoring incentive tied to Section 232 tariffs
articles.summary: Trump signed a proclamation on 20 July directing Commerce to establish an incentive programme under the Section 232 framework; companies committing to build, refurbish, or expand US primary aluminium smelting capacity and beginning construction by January 2029 may import primary aluminium at half the standard Section 232 tariff rate.
articles.novelis_angle: Expanded domestic US primary supply would ease P1020 availability and premium pressure for Novelis’s North American rolling operations, including the commissioning Bay Minette facility.

articles.title: US Customs auctions 12,000 tonnes of seized Chinese aluminium profiles in Riverside, California
articles.summary: US CBP is today auctioning approximately 12,000 tonnes of extruded aluminium profiles seized from China Zhongwang Holdings, material imported in 2017 and disguised as non-functional pallets to evade US anti-dumping tariffs; this is the second and final auction in the decade-long fraud case, with the material stored in 623 shipping containers in Riverside, California.
articles.novelis_angle: The resolution of this trade fraud case demonstrates sustained US enforcement against Chinese aluminium circumvention, reinforcing the fair-competition environment for Novelis’s expanding North American flat-rolled business.
